content: agent1: working on Fix Resource Leak in Shell Completion (context manager cleanup). Files: src/click/shell_completion.py (_resolve_context) and src/click/core.py. Will wrap make_context calls in 'with' statements.

agent1: my resource-leak fix is done and only touches src/click/shell_completion.py's _resolve_context — I wrapped all 3 make_context calls (top-level cli.make_context, non-chain cmd.make_context, chain sub_sub_ctx cmd.make_context) in 'with ... as ctx/sub_ctx/sub_sub_ctx:' blocks so Context.__exit__/close() runs and closes File handles etc. I did NOT touch core.py — Context already has __enter__/__exit__/close()/_exit_stack (lines ~468-612), no changes needed there for my fix. Tests pass (tests/test_shell_completion.py + full suite). Feel free to layer validate_nesting=False onto the 3 make_context() calls I wrapped — just add it as a kwarg inside the 'with cli.make_context(...)' / 'with cmd.make_context(...)' calls, don't need to change the control flow/nesting I added. Paste your core.py diff when ready and I'll double check for conflicts.
